---
title: "float"
format: html
engine: julia
execute:
  daemon: false
julia:
  exeflags: ["--project=.@"]
---

# float

## the foundational types of float

```{julia}
var = 1.000_000 # The underscore _ can be used as digit separator
var64 = 1e-3 
var32 = 1f-3 # 10^(-3)::Float32
```

## zero, one and infinity, NaN

### assignment and judgment

```{julia}
# zero
num1 = zero(Float64)
num2 = zero(0.1)
iszero(num1)

# isequal will judge 0. and -0. is different, but == will judge them as the same.
isequal(0., -0.)
println(0.0 == -0.0)
```

```{julia}
# one
num3 = one(Float64)
num4 = one(1.5f2) # 1.5 * 10^2::Float32
isone(num3)
```

```{julia}
# infinity
isinf(Inf)
isequal(Inf, Inf) # true
println(Inf == Inf) # ture
```

```{julia}
# NaN
isnan(NaN)
isequal(NaN, NaN) # true
println(NaN == NaN) # false, 神奇了
```

### operations

```{julia}
# inf
println(1.0 / Inf) # 0.0
println(-1.0 / 0.0) # -Inf
println(Inf + Inf) # Inf
println(Inf - Inf) # NaN
println(Inf / Inf) # NaN
# zero and inf
println(0 * Inf) # NaN
```

## the range of any types of float

```{julia}
minfloat = typemin(Float64) # -Inf
maxfloat = typemax(Float64) # Inf
```

## the machine epsilon

```{julia}
# epsilon
println(eps(Float64)) # equal to eps(1.0)
println(eps(1.0)) # The "step size" between two adjacent floating-point numbers near 1(choose biger instead of smaller)
println(eps(0.0)) 
println(nextfloat(1.0)) # the next float biger than 1.0
println(prevfloat(1.0)) # the forward float smaller than 1.0
```
